title: Extensive PostgreSQL Chaos Experiment
description: Comprehensive chaos experiment combining multiple PostgreSQL failure scenarios (Cache Miss, Vacuum Starvation, Temp Spill, Lock Storm, Pool Exhaustion, Query Saturation, Slow Transactions), Disaster Recovery (Primary Failover), and Infrastructure Chaos (CPU Stress, Network Latency). Supports S/M/L/XL sizing via environment variables. Includes intermediate steady-state validation, scenario-specific probes, observability validation, and comprehensive rollback/cleanup actions.

metadata.description: Comprehensive baseline metrics configuration for extensive postgres chaos experiment covering all failure scenarios

metrics.notes: Measures disk I/O activity for cache effectiveness. Increases significantly on cache misses.
Cache hit ratio as percentage. High precision for cache effectiveness monitoring.
Counts exclusive locks. Rare in normal operation, needs high threshold (3.0 sigma).
Number of active sessions. Increases under lock contention and saturation.
Rate of query cancellations due to conflicts. Sensitive indicator of lock issues.
Pool connection count. Direct indicator of pool exhaustion risk.
Transaction commit rate. Decreases under saturation due to contention.
Rate of tuples returned from queries. Reflects query execution efficiency.
Primary data lag in bytes. Critical metric for replication health.
Replication slot lag in bytes. Indicates WAL backlog on primary.
Synchronous replication status change rate. Indicates replication stability.
Cumulative count of slow queries (>1 second). Increases with slow transaction injection.
Rate of total query execution time in milliseconds. Sensitive to slow transactions.
Mean execution time per query in milliseconds. Increases with slow transactions.
Temporary file usage in bytes. Indicator of memory-to-disk spilling.
Rate of temporary file creation in bytes per second.
Cumulative count of temporary files created. Direct indicator of spill operations.
Rate of vacuum operations per minute. Decreases when vacuum is delayed or starved.
Heap scanned during vacuum operations. Reflects vacuum starvation when low.
Rate of analyze operations per minute. Correlates with vacuum activity.
